skill.name: diagnostic-action
skill.description: Guides an assistant through diagnostic reasoning for symptoms, failures, debugging questions, and incident explanations.
skill.summary: Structured root cause analysis for observed problems and unclear failures.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive; the line is descriptive metadata and contains no cryptographic operation. The system-reconnaissance finding at SKILL.md:13 is also a false positive; the line is an example diagnostic question and does not request host inspection, command execution, or data collection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3, but the line only describes when to use the skill for diagnostic questions. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, weak algorithms, or cryptographic instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown front matter text. There is no code or instruction related to cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:13, but the line is only a sample user question: "Why did this happen?" No evidence found of system enumeration, host inspection, external commands, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a natural language example for root cause analysis. It does not instruct the assistant to inspect the system or gather sensitive data.

content.value_statement: Unclear failures waste time when symptoms, causes, and evidence are mixed together. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to explain likely causes with ranked hypotheses and missing data.
actual_capabilities: Restates observed symptoms and scope before analysis.
Generates multiple plausible hypotheses for a reported problem.
Explains the mechanism behind each likely cause.
Ranks causes by likelihood and impact.
Identifies missing data needed to distinguish between causes.
Separates diagnostic explanation from recommendations unless asked.
limitations: Does not prescribe fixes unless the user explicitly asks.
Does not run commands or collect system data by itself.
Depends on the quality and completeness of user-provided evidence.
May need domain context to rank causes accurately.

use_cases.title: Debug a Failing Feature
use_cases.description: Analyze symptoms, logs, and recent changes to identify likely technical causes.

use_cases.title: Explain an Incident
use_cases.description: Turn an outage or service degradation report into ranked causal hypotheses.

use_cases.title: Clarify Business Process Failures
use_cases.description: Reason through why a workflow, handoff, or decision produced an unexpected result.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Simple Problem
prompt_templates.prompt: Use diagnostic-action to explain why this happened. Symptoms: [describe what changed]. Scope: [where it occurs]. Evidence: [what you know].

prompt_templates.title: Rank Possible Causes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use diagnostic-action to generate ranked hypotheses for this issue. Include mechanism, evidence for and against, confidence, and missing data.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Use diagnostic-action to analyze this incident timeline. Separate symptoms from causes, rank likely causes, and list data needed to confirm them.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge a Root Cause Theory
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use diagnostic-action to evaluate this proposed root cause. Identify weak evidence, alternate hypotheses, hidden assumptions, and decisive follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Why did our deploy succeed but the feature stayed unavailable for some users?
output_examples.output: The response would separate deploy success from feature availability, then rank causes such as rollout targeting, cache delay, configuration drift, or client version mismatch.

output_examples.input: Our support queue spiked after a billing change. What likely caused it?
output_examples.output: The response would define the affected scope, compare billing rule changes against user segments, and list evidence needed to isolate the strongest cause.

output_examples.input: The test suite became flaky after a dependency update.
output_examples.output: The response would rank hypotheses such as timing changes, environment differences, transitive dependency behavior, or shared state between tests.

best_practices: Provide symptoms, timeline, scope, and recent changes in the first request.
Ask for evidence for and against each hypothesis when stakes are high.
Use the missing data section to guide follow-up investigation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for fixes when you only need a diagnostic explanation.
Do not treat the highest-ranked hypothesis as proven without evidence.
Do not omit scope details such as affected users, systems, or time windows.

faq.question: What does this skill help diagnose?
faq.answer: It helps explain observed failures, bugs, incidents, and unclear outcomes by ranking likely causes.

faq.question: Does it fix the problem?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on diagnosis and only provides fixes if the user explicitly asks.

faq.question: Can it run debugging commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a reasoning guide and does not include command execution instructions.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide symptoms, scope, timeline, recent changes, known evidence, and what has already been checked.

faq.question: How does it handle uncertainty?
faq.answer: It ranks hypotheses by likelihood and impact, then lists missing data that would clarify the cause.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
